response: Founded in 1861, the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) is ranked as the number one university in the world. With an acceptance rate of just 3.9%, it is highly selective, catering to a student body of approximately 11,500. The university boasts an impressive endowment of $24.6 billion and charges an annual tuition of $57,590. MIT's faculty includes 1,069 members, and its community has produced 98 Nobel laureates, underscoring its reputation for excellence in education and research.
